proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with `feature_009 == 1` have more negative `dependency_BRAF` scores than cell lines with `feature_009 == 0`.
Cell lines with `feature_022 == 1` have more negative `dependency_EGFR` scores than cell lines with `feature_022 == 0`.
Cell lines with `feature_024 == 1` have more negative `dependency_ERBB2` scores than cell lines with `feature_024 == 0`.
Cell lines with `feature_014 == 1` have more negative `dependency_KRAS` scores than cell lines with `feature_014 == 0`.

analyses.code: Welch two-sample comparison of dependency_BRAF for feature_009 == 1 versus all other cell lines.
analyses.result_summary: `dependency_BRAF` was much more negative in `feature_009 == 1` lines: mean -0.805 versus -0.250, signed effect -0.555.

analyses.code: Welch two-sample comparison of dependency_EGFR for feature_022 == 1 versus all other cell lines.
analyses.result_summary: `dependency_EGFR` was more negative in `feature_022 == 1` lines: mean -0.601 versus -0.252, signed effect -0.349.

analyses.code: Welch two-sample comparison of dependency_ERBB2 for feature_024 == 1 versus all other cell lines.
analyses.result_summary: `dependency_ERBB2` was more negative in `feature_024 == 1` lines: mean -0.686 versus -0.253, signed effect -0.432.

analyses.code: Welch two-sample comparison of dependency_KRAS for feature_014 == 1 versus all other cell lines.
analyses.result_summary: `dependency_KRAS` was more negative in `feature_014 == 1` lines: mean -0.559 versus -0.252, signed effect -0.307.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with `feature_027 == 1` have more negative `dependency_RIT1` scores than cell lines with `feature_027 == 0`.
Increasing numeric `feature_016` is associated with more negative `dependency_POLR2A` scores.
Cell lines with `feature_018 == 1` have more negative `dependency_RPL11` scores than cell lines with `feature_018 == 0`.

analyses.code: Welch two-sample comparison of dependency_RIT1 for feature_027 == 1 versus all other cell lines.
analyses.result_summary: `dependency_RIT1` was more negative in `feature_027 == 1` lines: mean -0.353 versus -0.257, signed effect -0.096.

analyses.code: Ordinary least-squares regression dependency_POLR2A ~ feature_016.
analyses.result_summary: Higher `feature_016` strongly predicted more negative `dependency_POLR2A`; slope -0.305 dependency-score units per one-unit increase.

analyses.code: Welch two-sample comparison of dependency_RPL11 for feature_018 == 1 versus all other cell lines.
analyses.result_summary: `dependency_RPL11` was more negative in `feature_018 == 1` lines: mean -0.361 versus -0.252, signed effect -0.109.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Colorectal cell lines (`feature_011 == colorectal`) have more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than non-colorectal cell lines.
Cell lines with `feature_035 == 1` have more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than cell lines with `feature_035 == 0`.
Cell lines in the top quintile of `feature_028` (`feature_028 >= 0.98`) have more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than other cell lines.

analyses.code: Welch two-sample comparison of dependency_KIF18A for feature_011 == 'colorectal' versus all other lineages.
analyses.result_summary: Colorectal lines had substantially more negative `dependency_KIF18A`: mean -0.720 versus -0.252, signed effect -0.468.

analyses.code: Welch two-sample comparison of dependency_KIF18A for feature_035 == 1 versus feature_035 == 0.
analyses.result_summary: `feature_035 == 1` lines had more negative `dependency_KIF18A`: mean -0.545 versus -0.253, signed effect -0.292.

analyses.code: Welch two-sample comparison of dependency_KIF18A for feature_028 >= 0.98 versus lower feature_028.
analyses.result_summary: Top-quintile `feature_028` lines had more negative `dependency_KIF18A`: mean -0.524 versus -0.253, signed effect -0.271.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The complete subgroup `feature_011 == colorectal`, `feature_035 == 1`, and `feature_028 >= 0.98` has more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than all other cell lines, beyond any single feature alone.
Within the `feature_011 == colorectal`, `feature_035 == 1`, `feature_028 >= 0.98` subgroup, `feature_020 == 0` enables stronger `dependency_KIF18A`, whereas `feature_020 == 1` suppresses it.

analyses.code: Systematic predicate search over feature-value and quantile predicates; test subgroup feature_011 == 'colorectal' & feature_035 == 1 & feature_028 >= 0.98 versus complement.
analyses.result_summary: The three-predicate KIF18A subgroup was much more dependent than the complement: mean -1.183 versus -0.253, signed effect -0.930.

analyses.code: Within the refined KIF18A subgroup, compare feature_020 == 0 subset against all other cell lines and separately inspect feature_020 == 1 subset.
analyses.result_summary: Adding `feature_020 == 0` strengthened the KIF18A subgroup sharply: n=2387, mean -1.379 versus -0.253, signed effect -1.126. The same colorectal/feature_035/feature_028-high subgroup with `feature_020 == 1` had mean -0.253 and did not show stronger dependency.

proposed_hypotheses.text: For `dependency_BRAF`, systematic subgroup search will not find a stronger complete dependency subgroup than the primary `feature_009 == 1` signal.
For `dependency_EGFR`, dependency is concentrated in `feature_022 == 1` lines and may be slightly stronger in `feature_016 >= 0.964` and `feature_011 == brain` cell lines.

analyses.code: Systematic BRAF heterogeneity search over one-, two-, and three-predicate subgroups.
analyses.result_summary: The best interpretable BRAF subgroup remained `feature_009 == 1`; combinations with additional predicates did not materially improve on the signed effect -0.555 without greatly reducing support.

analyses.code: Systematic EGFR heterogeneity search over one-, two-, and three-predicate subgroups.
analyses.result_summary: The strongest EGFR-enriched subgroup found was `feature_022 == 1`, `feature_016 >= 0.964`, and `feature_011 == brain`: mean -0.658 versus -0.278, signed effect -0.381. The broader `feature_022 == 1` signal remained the main driver.

proposed_hypotheses.text: For `dependency_ERBB2`, systematic subgroup search will identify `feature_024 == 1` as the primary complete dependency subgroup.
For `dependency_KRAS`, systematic subgroup search will identify `feature_014 == 1` as the primary complete dependency subgroup.
For `dependency_RIT1`, systematic subgroup search will identify `feature_027 == 1` as a weaker but significant dependency subgroup.

analyses.code: Systematic ERBB2 heterogeneity search over feature-value and quantile-predicate subgroups; primary supported predicate tested as feature_024 == 1.
analyses.result_summary: `feature_024 == 1` remained the dominant ERBB2 subgroup: mean -0.686 versus -0.253, signed effect -0.432.

analyses.code: Systematic KRAS heterogeneity search over feature-value and quantile-predicate subgroups; primary supported predicate tested as feature_014 == 1.
analyses.result_summary: `feature_014 == 1` remained the dominant KRAS subgroup: mean -0.559 versus -0.252, signed effect -0.307.

analyses.code: Systematic RIT1 heterogeneity search over feature-value and quantile-predicate subgroups; primary supported predicate tested as feature_027 == 1.
analyses.result_summary: `feature_027 == 1` was a significant but smaller RIT1 dependency subgroup: mean -0.353 versus -0.257, signed effect -0.096.

proposed_hypotheses.text: `dependency_TMED10` has no large discrete oncogene-like dependency subgroup, but high `feature_016` and high `feature_026` in `feature_010 == luminal` lines are associated with modestly more negative `dependency_TMED10`.
`dependency_TMED10` is relatively less negative in the subgroup `feature_016 <= 0.66`, `feature_026 <= 0.919`, and `feature_027 == 1`.

analyses.code: Systematic TMED10 heterogeneity search over one-, two-, and three-predicate subgroups.
analyses.result_summary: The strongest negative TMED10 subgroup found was `feature_016 >= 0.999`, `feature_026 >= 1.08`, and `feature_010 == luminal`: mean -0.338 versus -0.254, signed effect -0.084.

analyses.code: Systematic TMED10 heterogeneity search for protective/resistant subgroups.
analyses.result_summary: The relatively resistant TMED10 subgroup `feature_016 <= 0.66`, `feature_026 <= 0.919`, `feature_027 == 1` had mean -0.166 versus -0.254, signed effect +0.089.

proposed_hypotheses.text: `dependency_DHX9` is modestly more negative in the subgroup `feature_016 >= 0.964`, `feature_023 >= 5.669`, and `feature_026 >= 1.101`.
`dependency_POLR2A` is more negative in the subgroup `feature_016 >= 0.999`, `feature_010 == adenocarcinoma`, and `feature_003 >= 0.428`.

analyses.code: Systematic DHX9 heterogeneity search over one-, two-, and three-predicate subgroups.
analyses.result_summary: The DHX9-enriched subgroup `feature_016 >= 0.964`, `feature_023 >= 5.669`, `feature_026 >= 1.101` had mean -0.400 versus -0.333, signed effect -0.067.

analyses.code: Systematic POLR2A heterogeneity search over one-, two-, and three-predicate subgroups.
analyses.result_summary: The POLR2A-enriched subgroup `feature_016 >= 0.999`, `feature_010 == adenocarcinoma`, `feature_003 >= 0.428` had mean -0.558 versus -0.418, signed effect -0.139.

proposed_hypotheses.text: `dependency_RPL11` is more negative in a high-feature subgroup defined by `feature_003 >= 0.548`, `feature_016 >= 0.964`, and `feature_023 >= 5.85`.
The screen batch column `feature_005` does not explain the main dependency signals as strongly as the biological feature subgroups.

analyses.code: Systematic RPL11 heterogeneity search over one-, two-, and three-predicate subgroups.
analyses.result_summary: The RPL11-enriched subgroup `feature_003 >= 0.548`, `feature_016 >= 0.964`, `feature_023 >= 5.85` had mean -0.409 versus -0.266, signed effect -0.142.

analyses.code: Compare top dependency effects against feature_005 batch levels discovered in the screen.
analyses.result_summary: The dominant dependency effects were attached to outcome-specific molecular/lineage features such as `feature_009`, `feature_022`, `feature_024`, `feature_014`, and the KIF18A subgroup, not to `feature_005` batch labels.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Final best-supported BRAF dependency subgroup: `feature_009 == 1` cell lines have more negative `dependency_BRAF` scores.

Final best-supported EGFR dependency subgroup: `feature_022 == 1` cell lines, with possible enrichment when `feature_016 >= 0.964` and `feature_011 == brain`, have more negative `dependency_EGFR` scores.
Final best-supported ERBB2 dependency subgroup: `feature_024 == 1` cell lines have more negative `dependency_ERBB2` scores.
Final best-supported KRAS dependency subgroup: `feature_014 == 1` cell lines have more negative `dependency_KRAS` scores.
Final best-supported RIT1 dependency subgroup: `feature_027 == 1` cell lines have more negative `dependency_RIT1` scores.

Final best-supported KIF18A dependency subgroup: `feature_011 == colorectal`, `feature_035 == 1`, `feature_028 >= 0.98`, and `feature_020 == 0` cell lines have strongly more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores.
Final best-supported TMED10 dependency subgroup is modest: `feature_016 >= 0.999`, `feature_026 >= 1.08`, and `feature_010 == luminal` cell lines have more negative `dependency_TMED10` scores.
Final best-supported DHX9 dependency subgroup is modest: `feature_016 >= 0.964`, `feature_023 >= 5.669`, and `feature_026 >= 1.101` cell lines have more negative `dependency_DHX9` scores.

Final best-supported POLR2A dependency subgroup: high `feature_016` lines, especially `feature_016 >= 0.999`, `feature_010 == adenocarcinoma`, and `feature_003 >= 0.428`, have more negative `dependency_POLR2A` scores.
Final best-supported RPL11 dependency subgroup: `feature_003 >= 0.548`, `feature_016 >= 0.964`, and `feature_023 >= 5.85` cell lines have more negative `dependency_RPL11` scores.

analyses.code: Final synthesis of main-effect tests and systematic one-, two-, and three-predicate dependency heterogeneity searches for every dependency outcome.
analyses.result_summary: The strongest final signal was the KIF18A four-predicate subgroup (`feature_011 == colorectal`, `feature_035 == 1`, `feature_028 >= 0.98`, `feature_020 == 0`; effect -1.126). BRAF, EGFR, ERBB2, KRAS, and RIT1 were dominated by single binary features. TMED10, DHX9, POLR2A, and RPL11 showed broader quantitative-feature heterogeneity rather than large oncogene-like single-feature effects.
